purpose: Normalized high-confidence deterministic candidates extracted from rigorous code/data sources. These are not product rules.

items: let me know if you have any questions
let me know if you have any further questions
let me know if you have any other questions
if you have any more questions, feel free to ask
if you have any more questions, please don't hesitate to ask
groups.deterministic_rule_candidate: Report at paragraph or sentence start, or report anywhere for explicit AI identity and knowledge-cutoff leakage.
fixture_ideas: Chat assistant answer ending with a follow-up offer.
Knowledge cutoff disclaimer in first sentence.
Support article that legitimately says a product has a knowledge cutoff as a concept, for no-hit.
groups.false_positive_risk: Polite human email endings and documentation about AI systems can contain some forms. Require start/end position or AI self-reference for default severity.

groups.deterministic_rule_candidate: Report invisible Unicode controls outside code spans and allow explicit no-hits for bidirectional-language examples.
fixture_ideas: Markdown paragraph with a zero-width space in ordinary prose.
Security note intentionally naming U+202E without embedding it, for no-hit.
groups.false_positive_risk: Some marks are legitimate in multilingual text, pasted legal text, and Unicode documentation.

items: not just X, but also Y
not only X, but also Y
it is not X, it is Y
it is not X; it is Y
X is not the problem. Y is.
the question is not X. it is Y.
stops being X and starts being Y
groups.deterministic_rule_candidate: Report repeated pivots per document, or a single highly formulaic paragraph with two or more variants.
fixture_ideas: LinkedIn-style paragraph with three negative pivots.
Literary antithesis used once in an essay, for no-hit.
groups.false_positive_risk: Antithesis is a normal rhetorical device. Single-use matches should be source-only or low severity.

groups.candidate_type: phrase or lemmatized trigram with narrative density gate
items: breath she didn't know she was holding
breath he didn't know he was holding
groups.deterministic_rule_candidate: Use as density cues in narrative prose, not as standalone errors.
fixture_ideas: Generated romance/fantasy scene with five cue families in 300 words.
Medical or sports paragraph using breath/heart words literally, for no-hit.
groups.false_positive_risk: Many items are ordinary fiction actions. Density, domain, and co-occurrence are required.

items: at the end of the day
groups.deterministic_rule_candidate: Report only at section starts, paragraph starts, or when repeated as scaffolding.
fixture_ideas: SEO article opening with time-based framing.
Speaker transcript using 'here's the thing' once naturally, for no-hit.
groups.false_positive_risk: Some phrases are normal speech. Position and repetition matter.

groups.candidate_type: phrase plus missing local citation evidence
groups.deterministic_rule_candidate: Report when phrase appears without nearby source, named study, linked citation, or concrete number.
fixture_ideas: Health/SEO paragraph with uncited studies-show claim.
Academic paragraph that names a study and includes a citation, for no-hit.
groups.false_positive_risk: Valid summaries can use these phrases when evidence is nearby. Needs citation-aware gating.
